FISHMAN LIVING TRUST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ALL CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN PLAINTIFF KATHLEEN SAFFELL'S COMPLAINT ARE DENIED IN THEIR ENTIRETY. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-08-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Off calendar. The City has been dismissed as a defendant and it filed a dismissal of its cross-complaint. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James V. Weixel, Esq. of Demler Armstrong & Rowland, LLP, Tel. 415.949.1900, for defendant and moving party;
Brian Graziani, Esq. of Sisneros Graziani LLP, Tel. 415.367.3679, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JAMES M. FISHMAN LIVING TRUST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and alternative motion for summary adjudication of all causes of action alleged in plaintiff Kathleen Saffell's complaint are denied in their entirety. The Trust's motion for summary judgment as to City and County of San Francisco's cross-complaint is off calendar in light of the City's dismissal of its cross-complaint. The Trust failed to satisfy its initial burden of showing it did not owe a duty to Ms. Saffell. It has not shown the alleged dangerous condition was trivial as a matter of law per Stathoulis v. City of Montebello (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 559, 566-567. The Trust's reliance solely on Ms. Saffell's deposition testimony that she was unable to determine whether there were more or fewer leaves than those depicted in a photograph taken after her fall, without more, does not show that there were an insufficient amount of leaves to impede Ms. Saffell's view of the uneven sidewalk. Even if the Trust satisfied its initial burden, there is a triable issue whether Ms. Saffell was able to see the alleged hazard because of the leaves on the sidewalk. (Plaintiff's Additional Material Fact 38-40.) The Trust's evidentiary objection 6 is overruled. The Trust's argument that the sidewalk defect was obvious and open lacks merit because the Trust has a duty to take precautions other than a warning to protect against the risk of harm from an obviously unsafe condition, even if it does not have a duty to warn. (Osborn v. Mission Ready Mix (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 104, 121-122.) Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.
